parts.heading: Introduction
parts.text: The Risk & Compliance area documents the control environment, regulatory obligations, known gaps, and audit history for the Bank Guarantee Issuance process. Bank guarantee issuance sits at the intersection of trade finance, credit risk, AML/sanctions compliance, and prudential reporting: a guarantee is an off-balance-sheet contingent credit commitment that can expose the bank to financial crime risk, capital mis-reporting, and regulatory sanction if the process is not tightly controlled. This area therefore directly shapes the bank's standing with BaFin, the EBA, and internal audit, and its completeness matters as much as the operational process it oversees.

parts.heading: Current state
parts.text:
The controls section documents five controls spanning the process's key risk points. Three are preventive: CP-BGID-001 (Four-Eyes Issuance Approval) requires a Trade Finance Manager's sign-off before any guarantee is released, with a Head of Trade Finance escalation above EUR 5 million; CP-BGID-002 (Sanctions Screening) screens every beneficiary and beneficiary country against EU and UN sanctions lists before approval is sought; and CP-BGID-003 (Facility Limit Check) is a fully automated system block that prevents issuance when the client's available guarantee facility is insufficient, enforcing the CRR large-exposure ceiling at the transaction level. A fourth preventive control, CP-BGID-004 (Collateral Block Confirmation), is performed manually by the Trade Finance Officer for every partially-secured guarantee, confirming that cash collateral has been received and blocked before the issuance approval step. The fifth control, CP-BGID-005 (Sanctions Screening Hit Rationale Capture), is detective rather than preventive: it requires a Compliance Analyst to complete a non-nullable written rationale field in the TFS screening workflow before a sanctions hit can be marked cleared. This control is still in implementation, remediated directly from OAF-BGID-003, with a target live date of Q4 2026.

The regulatory landscape mapped against this process is substantial at twelve elements spanning four domains. The AML and financial crime domain is anchored by REG-BGID-001 (AMLD5/AMLR), requiring CDD on applicants, beneficiary screening, and five-year document retention, and supplemented by REG-BGID-004 (EBA ML/TF Risk Factor Guidelines for trade finance), which extends the obligation to a product-specific ML/TF risk assessment. REG-BGID-011 (AML Record-Keeping under AMLD5 Art. 40 and AMLR Art. 77) adds a machine-readable format requirement and a harmonised retention rule that comes into force with the AMLR from July 2027. The prudential domain is covered by REG-BGID-003 (CRR Art. 111, 100% credit conversion factor for credit-substitute guarantees), REG-BGID-005 (CRR Art. 395, 25% Tier 1 large-exposure limit), and REG-BGID-006 (CRR Art. 403, mandatory substitution for collateralised exposures), all of which interact directly with CP-BGID-003 and CP-BGID-004. Two governance regulations — REG-BGID-007 (MaRisk BTO 1.2) and REG-BGID-008 (EBA/GL/2021/05 Internal Governance) — mandate the four-eyes and segregation-of-duties arrangements that CP-BGID-001 implements, and these were the direct basis for two closed findings. REG-BGID-002 (URDG 758) governs the form of the guarantee instrument itself. REG-BGID-009 (DORA, applicable from January 2025) requires the Trade Finance System to sit within the bank's ICT risk management framework. REG-BGID-010 (GDPR) governs personal data of corporate representatives collected at intake. REG-BGID-012 (EBA ITS COREP C 07.00) mandates quarterly supervisory reporting of off-balance-sheet guarantee exposures.

The single control gap, CG-BGID-001, records that the Corporate Portal does not enforce mandatory field completion at application intake, leaving completeness checking as a manual task for the Trade Finance Officer. This gap carries LOW severity and is flagged as mitigated: downstream controls CP-BGID-003 and CP-BGID-001 are judged sufficient to catch material errors before issuance, and the gap is addressed in the target state by TD-BGID-003 (Smart Intake Portal with mandatory field enforcement). No additional As-Is control is planned.

Three audit findings round out the area. OAF-BGID-001 and OAF-BGID-002 are both closed. The first, a LOW-severity internal audit finding from March 2024, identified one case in a sample of 25 — 4% — where a Trade Finance Officer who submitted a guarantee application also recorded the issuance approval in TFS, breaching CP-BGID-001; remediation via a TFS role-based workflow constraint was verified by Q3 2024. The second, a MEDIUM-severity finding from the September 2023 BaFin Sonderprüfung, found that TFS recorded the four-eyes approval using a server timestamp rather than the authenticated officer's sign-off time, undermining the audit trail's integrity under MaRisk BTO 1.2; the enhancement was delivered and verified by BaFin by Q1 2024. The third finding, OAF-BGID-003, is open and rated HIGH severity: a Q1 2025 internal audit review of 40 applications found that 6 cases — 15% — where the Sanctions Screening Tool returned a potential hit contained no contemporaneous written analyst rationale in the application record, contrary to AMLD5 Art. 40 requirements. CP-BGID-005 is the direct remediation vehicle; its TFS enhancement has a target live date of Q4 2026.

parts.heading: What stands out
parts.text:
The area's strongest feature is the explicit linkage between controls and their regulatory anchors: all five controls trace to specific regulations, and the two closed audit findings show a bank that responded to regulatory challenge with targeted, system-enforced improvements rather than procedural workarounds. The full automation of CP-BGID-003 at the credit-exposure chokepoint is a genuine strength, and the four-eyes escalation threshold in CP-BGID-001 reflects a proportionate risk appetite for larger guarantee amounts.

What is missing or uncertain is significant, however. Six of the twelve regulation elements contain open confirmations with SMEs or with specific functions. REG-BGID-003 notes that capital calculation against guarantee exposures is "to be confirmed with SME." REG-BGID-004 states that the broader ML/TF risk assessment per application is "to be confirmed with the SME." REG-BGID-009 records that the Trade Finance System must be in the DORA ICT risk framework but does not confirm it is. REG-BGID-011 flags that the machine-readable retention format and 5-year purge schedule is "to be confirmed with Compliance and Records Management." REG-BGID-012 notes that COREP C 07.00 population from the TFS feed is "to be confirmed with Finance and Regulatory Reporting." This pattern means the "how it is met" narrative for half the regulatory inventory rests on stated intent rather than verified practice. OAF-BGID-003 remains a live HIGH-severity finding with at least six months of remediation runway, and the four primary preventive controls — CP-BGID-001 through CP-BGID-004 — all carry proposed rather than elicited provenance, meaning their accuracy has not yet been validated by the SME.

parts.heading: Recommendation
parts.text: The most urgent action is to close OAF-BGID-003 on schedule: the TFS sanctions rationale enforcement field must reach production by Q4 2026 and be verified by internal audit, since each dismissal without a written rationale is a live AMLD5 Art. 40 breach. Running in parallel, the open "to be confirmed" clauses across REG-BGID-003, REG-BGID-004, REG-BGID-009, REG-BGID-011, and REG-BGID-012 should be resolved through a targeted session with Compliance, Finance, and Regulatory Reporting, converting stated intentions into verified controls or surfacing new gaps that will need to be documented. Finally, the four preventive controls that carry only proposed provenance — CP-BGID-001, CP-BGID-002, CP-BGID-003, and CP-BGID-004 — should be moved to elicited through a control-compliance specialist review, as these are the process's primary risk safeguards and their accuracy cannot be assumed without SME confirmation.
